pi_items.source: task description and export_result.sh script path checks
setup_task.sh SQL inserts for MMSI 1001 (Anchorage, Terminal, Anchorage)
setup_task.sh SQL inserts for MMSI 1003 (only 1 ping, filtered by ">1 ping" rule)
setup_task.sh SQL inserts - only MMSI 1001 has Terminal stay within 24h of Anchorage stay

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated maritime AIS data injected into an Oracle database via setup_task.sh. The expected output CSV file path is explicitly "/home/ga/Documents/exports/congestion_report.csv". Based on the injected synthetic pings, the target metric expectations correctly require exactly 3 vessel stays for MMSI 1001 (two Anchorage stays and one Terminal stay), 0 valid stays for MMSI 1003 (filtered out due to a single ping), and exactly 1 valid port call (MMSI 1001), while MMSI 1002 is correctly rejected for exceeding the 24-hour linkage constraint.
